channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: As Davos 2025 reacts to Trump tack, EU chief leads defense against climate change
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — As the World Economic Forum’s annual gabfest in the Swiss Alps got into full swing on Tuesday, U.S. President Donald Trump gave everybody something to talk about with his actions on his first day back in office.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: Russia sanctions ‘likely’ if Moscow doesn’t negotiate Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: President Trump said Tuesday it was likely he would impose additional sanctions on Russia if it did not come to the negotiating table to broker a ceasefire in Ukraine. "Sounds likely," Trump said of potential sanctions on Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Israel's top general resigns over Oct. 7 failures, adding to pressure on Netanyahu
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's top general resigned Tuesday, taking responsibility for security failures tied to Hamas' surprise attack that triggered the war in Gaza and adding to pressure on Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who has delayed any public inquiry that could potentially implicate his leade
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and AREF TUFAHA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel launches a major West Bank operation, days after Gaza ceasefire
item.description: Israel launched a major military operation Tuesday in the occupied West Bank city of Jenin that killed at least nine Palestinians and left at least 40 more people wounded, Palestinian health officials said, as Israel’s fragile ceasefire with Hamas in Gaza entered its third day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stefanik vows to keep the U.S. engaged at U.N. to counter China
item.description: Rep. Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) assured skeptical Senate Democrats during her confirmation hearing as President Trump's pick for U.N. ambassador that she is committed to engaging in the United Nations, saying it’s a priority venue to challenge China’s push for dominance on the global stage.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Rescue camera gave officials a view of hundreds of miners in South Africa trapped in shaft
item.description: Late last year, a specialized mine rescue camera was lowered into an almost 2.6-kilometer (1.6-mile) deep mineshaft in South Africa, where hundreds of illegal miners were reported to be trapped, starving, dehydrated and desperate to get out.
item.dc:creator: Mogomotsi Magome, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Putin, Xi vow to ‘deepen’ alliance hours after Trump re-enters the White House
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin held a video call hours after President Donald Trump re-entered the White House and pledged to "deepen" their partnership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colombia's president will declare an emergency over deadly ELN attacks in the northeast
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Colombian President Gustavo Petro said Monday that he will declare a state of emergency over the guerrilla attacks in the northeast that have killed dozens of people and forced thousands to flee their homes.
item.dc:creator: MANUEL RUEDA and ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russian officials reaffirmed their commitment to the neutrality of the critical trade route and said it expects both America and Panama to continue respecting that agreement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Russia sounds off on Trump’s threat to retake the Panama Canal
item.description: Russia’s foreign ministry has called on Trump to reaffirm the current international agreement surrounding the Panama Canal and to leave it in control of the nation of Panama.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio's first order of business: Meeting Quad's foreign secretaries
item.description: Newly sworn-in Secretary of State Marco Rubio is hitting the ground running Tuesday with meetings with his counterparts in the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue, known as the Quad, which includes India, Australia and Japan, a bloc formed to counter Chinese clout in the Indo-Pacific.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: Mexico defends sovereignty as US seeks to label cartels as terrorists
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum said Tuesday that President Donald Trump's executive order moving toward designating drug cartels as foreign terrorist organizations would only impact Mexico if there's close coordination between the two governments.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: Putin 'destroying Russia' with war in Ukraine
item.description: President Trump said Russian President Vladimir Putin is “destroying Russia” with its nearly three-year war in Ukraine, adding he hopes the Kremlin leader is open to making a deal to end the conflict. "He should make a deal.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: WHO defends global health, security role after Trump withdrawal
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) on Tuesday said it "regrets" President Trump's move to withdraw from the global health authority, saying it hopes the U.S. will reconsider the decision.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: Italian journalist detained in Iran says she expected to be held longer and praises Musk's role
item.description: ROME (AP) — An Italian journalist detained in Iran whose fate became intertwined with that of an Iranian engineer wanted by the United States said she assumed she would have been held much longer, and said her boyfriend's contact with Elon Musk might have been “fundamental” to her release.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 8 people die in a fire at a home for the elderly in Serbia, officials say
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Eight people died Monday in a fire at a home for older people on the outskirts of the Serbian capital, Belgrade, that is suspected of being set by a resident, officials said. Seven others were injured in the blaze that erupted around 3:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump wants to visit China, strengthen relationship: Report
item.description: The president-elect has vowed to impose tariffs and other mechanisms against China upon his return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Israel Defense Forces will receive hostages Sunday with equipped camper trailers and comforting supplies
item.description: The Israel Defense forces completed preparations Saturday to receive 33 hostages being held by Hama Sunday after a cease-fire deal was reached.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis hurts his right arm after falling for the second time in just over a month
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis fell Thursday and hurt his right arm, the Vatican said, just weeks after another apparent fall resulted in a bad bruise on his chin. Francis didn’t break his arm, but a sling was put on as a precaution, the Vatican spokesman said in a statement On Dec.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation
